reasoning.typography: Clean sans-serif system with strong weights. Bold headline creates presence, but nothing particularly distinctive — feels competent rather than characterful.
reasoning.hierarchy: The hero statement dominates well with underline emphasis on key phrase. Blog list is appropriately secondary, though date/author metadata competes slightly.
reasoning.distinctiveness: The thesis statement has specificity ('edge of large markets...technological and societal pressures'), but the overall structure is still 'About/People/Writing' standard. Green square logo is memorable but layout could work for many funds.
reasoning.color: Excellent restraint. Single green accent used sparingly for branding only. Black text on white, disciplined color discipline throughout.
reasoning.density: Confident whitespace around hero statement. Blog list has appropriate breathing room. Not overly padded or cramped — feels considered.
reasoning.voice: The IA is mostly conventional (About/People/Investments/Jobs/Writing) but the hero copy has an actual POV. The thesis gets prime real estate rather than buried. Still feels like professional services stencil overall.

winning_patterns: Ruthless color restraint — green only for brand identity, not decoration
Thesis statement gets hero treatment with meaningful underline on core concept
Content density feels professional and confident, not over-designed
Clean typographic hierarchy without resorting to multiple font families
anti_patterns: Navigation is pure boilerplate fund template (About/People/Investments/Jobs/Writing)
Blog post list is undifferentiated metadata soup — all posts look identical
Footer Contact section is procedural institutional copy
No typographic personality — could be any professional services firm's font stack
Recent posts have no visual differentiation or importance signaling
